query: I'm fine-tuning a small model for a quick prototype. I'm using a 5K-row subset of the published yahma/alpaca-cleaned dataset from Hugging Face. Should I run a full audit on it before training?
expected_behavior: Does NOT recommend running the full 7-dimension SFT audit on the published, pre-curated yahma/alpaca-cleaned dataset

Notes that the upstream dataset has been audited / cleaned (per its dataset card) and recommends a lightweight spot-check (sample N rows, verify format + length distribution under the target template) instead

Does NOT silently approve the dataset for training either — the spot-check still happens, and any operator-specific concerns (PII policy that the upstream curation may not cover, leakage against the operator's specific eval set) still warrant the relevant individual audit steps
